text:
American athlete Alison Gibson endured a moment to forget in theOlympic3m springboard contest after landing feet first in the water and recording a score of 0.0.

Competing in her second Olympics, Gibson was looking to secure her spot in the semi-finals of the 3m springboard contest.

However, the American’s campaign got off to the worst possible start when she suffered a scary moment with her very first dive of the preliminary event.

Attempting an inward two-and-a-half pike, Gibson caught her feet on the board as she spun down towards the water.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

The collision with the board caused the 25-year-old to lose all momentum as she collapsed feet-first into the water.

While Gibson emerged from the water and appeared able to continue, the horror dive saw her awarded zeroes from every judge for the effort.

‘I hit my heels and my feet on the board. I have cuts along the sides. And then I bruised my right heel pretty good, but I was determined to keep going,’ Gibson later said.

‘I definitely was in pain. But one thing that I say is I hope that anyone who is watching just was able to see what it looks like to get up and keep going even when things don’t go the way you want them to. It’s about the fight.’

OUCH ð¬A tough moment for Alison Gibson as her feet strike the board on the way down.#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/jdXFwTKcVh

Despite improving after her initial effort, Gibson’s eventual score of 198.3 left her bottom of the pile at the end of the preliminary stage, with only the top 16 advancing to the semi-finals.

‘Obviously, today didn’t go the way I wanted it to,’ she added.

‘But I think you can learn and grow from every experience, and I truly hope that the next generation of athletes recognise that even in the worst moments you can keep fighting, you can walk courageously.’

Team GBduo Grace Reid and Yasmin Harper, meanwhile, finished fifth and ninth respectively and will compete in Thursday morning’s semi-final.

Harper will look to collect her second medal of the games havingwon bronze in the women’s 3m synchronised event with Scarlett Mew Jensen.

China’s Yiwen Chen qualified in first place with a commanding score of 356.40, almost 20 points more than second place Maddison Keeney from Australia.

title: American athlete scores 0.0 after nightmare dive at Paris Olympics | Metro News
description: American athlete Alison Gibson endured a moment to forget in the Olympic 3m springboard contest after recording a score of 0.0.
